description: 31-slide executive deck: DALP XvP Real Estate Escrow for Ministry of Justice Qatar (with DALP screenshots)

slides.title: XvP Escrow for Real Estate
slides.subtitle: Tokenized Title Deed Transfer with DALP | Ministry of Justice Qatar | 2026

slides.title: Atomic Title Transfers
slides.body:
* Qatar real estate transfers currently involve 15-30 day settlement cycles, multiple intermediaries, and manual escrow processes that introduce delay, cost, and counterparty risk.
* DALP enables tokenization of title deeds as digital assets with embedded compliance controls, creating a verifiable on-chain representation of property ownership.
* The XvP (Exchange vs Payment) settlement addon ensures the real estate token and tokenized deposit exchange atomically: both legs settle simultaneously or neither does.
* Settlement time drops from weeks to minutes while maintaining full regulatory controls, identity verification, and audit trails.
* This proposal outlines how the Ministry of Justice can modernize property transfer using DALP's tokenization and atomic settlement capabilities.

slides.title: Real Estate Transfer Today
slides.left_body:
* Manual title deed registration
* 15-30 day settlement cycle
* Paper-based escrow process
* Multiple intermediaries required
* Reconciliation overhead
* Counterparty risk exposure
slides.right_body:
* Tokenized title deeds on-chain
* Atomic settlement in minutes
* Programmable escrow via XvP
* Single shared ledger
* Automated compliance checks
* Zero counterparty risk

slides.title: Tokenization Creates Value
slides.item_0:
Fractional Ownership
Enable partial ownership of real estate assets. Investors can hold token fractions representing proportional title deed interests.
slides.item_1:
Programmable Rules
Compliance and transfer controls are embedded directly in the token. Restrictions enforce themselves at every transaction.
slides.item_2:
Atomic Settlement
Simultaneous exchange of asset and payment with zero timing gap. No delivery-vs-payment mismatch possible.
slides.item_3:
Full Audit Trail
Every transfer, approval, and state change is recorded on-chain. Complete transparency for regulators and participants.

slides.title: Asset Designer
slides.body: DALP's Asset Designer enables tokenized real estate instruments with configurable compliance, metadata, and issuance parameters
slides.image_path: ../shared/brand/dalp-screenshots/04 - Asset Designer/Real Estate Tokenisation/jpg/02 Step Tokenised real Estate.jpg

slides.title: Cash Leg: Tokenized Deposits
slides.col1:
Tokenized Deposits
* Issued by a licensed bank
* Represent actual bank deposits
* Subject to banking regulation
* Deposit insurance applies
* Known issuer and legal recourse
* Suitable for regulated settlement
slides.col2:
Why Not Stablecoins
* Algorithmic or collateral-backed
* Variable regulatory status
* Limited legal recourse
* Not bank-supervised
* Unsuitable for institutional real estate settlement
* No deposit insurance coverage

slides.title: Exchange vs Payment
slides.body:
XvP ensures the asset leg (real estate token) and cash leg (tokenized deposit) exchange atomically. Either both legs settle or neither does.

This eliminates three critical risks:
* Delivery without payment, where the seller transfers the title deed but never receives funds
* Payment without delivery, where the buyer pays but never receives the deed
* Settlement timing mismatch, where one leg settles hours or days before the other

DALP's built-in XvP addon supports named settlements, multi-party approvals, configurable cutoff dates, auto-execution triggers, and HTLC hashlock for cross-chain settlement scenarios.

slides.title: XvP Settlement Dashboard
slides.body: Configure and monitor XvP settlements with multi-party approval tracking, settlement states, and execution controls

slides.title: Why XvP Escrow Works
slides.item1:
Atomic Exchange
Both legs settle simultaneously or not at all. No partial execution risk.
slides.item2:
Multi-Party Approval
All parties must approve before execution. Assets locked in contract.
slides.item3:
Programmable Conditions
Cutoff dates, auto-execute triggers, and compliance gates built in.
slides.item4:
Cancel Safeguards
Voting-based cancellation requires consensus. No unilateral withdrawal.
slides.item5:
Expiry Protection
Automatic asset return if settlement expires past the cutoff date.
slides.item6:
Cross-Chain Ready
HTLC hashlock support enables settlement across multiple ledgers.

slides.title: Settlement Journey
slides.step1_body: KYC/AML, identity verification, wallet provisioning for buyer, seller, and escrow agent
slides.step2_body: Tokenize title deed as real estate token, issue tokenized deposit, configure compliance
slides.step3_body: Create named XvP settlement with two flows: RE token and deposit transfer
slides.step4_body: Parties approve and lock assets. Atomic execution transfers both legs simultaneously. Title deed registry updated.

slides.title: Settlement State Machine
slides.body:
The XvP settlement follows a deterministic lifecycle:

* PENDING: Settlement created with named flows. Awaiting approvals from all senders.
* READY: All parties have approved and locked their assets in the settlement contract.
* EXECUTED: Atomic transfer complete. Both the real estate token and deposit have changed hands.
* CANCELLED: Multi-party vote to cancel succeeded. All locked assets returned to original holders.
* EXPIRED: Cutoff date passed without execution. Withdrawal of locked assets enabled for all parties.
* WAITING_SECRET (HTLC only): For cross-chain scenarios, the hashlock secret must be revealed before execution can proceed. If the secret is not revealed before timeout, the settlement expires.

slides.title: Roles and Responsibilities
slides.body: Key participants in the XvP real estate settlement and their responsibilities
table_data: Approves deposit lock, receives RE token
Approves RE token lock, receives deposit

slides.title: DALP Technology Stack
slides.layer1_body: Asset Designer, XvP Settlement Dashboard, Compliance Console, User Management
slides.layer2_body: Token Lifecycle Engine, XvP Settlement Factory, Identity Service (OnchainID)
slides.layer3_body: 12 Pluggable Modules: Country Lists, Identity Verification, Transfer Approval, Collateral
slides.layer4_body: Besu (Permissioned EVM), Smart Contracts, Event Indexing, Multi-chain Support
slides.layer5_body: Kubernetes, HSM Integration, Observability (Grafana, Loki, Tempo), API Gateway

slides.title: Smart Contract Components
slides.item_0:
DALPAsset Contract
Single configurable token for any instrument. Real estate token with compliance modules attached at deployment.
slides.item_1:
XvP Settlement Factory
Creates settlement instances. Configures flows, cutoff dates, hashlock, and auto-execute parameters.
slides.item_2:
Compliance Modules
Country Allow List, Identity Verification, Transfer Approval. Enforced at every transfer automatically.
slides.item_3:
Identity (OnchainID)
ERC-3643 compliant identity. KYC/AML claims, issuer verification, claim-based access control.

slides.title: Compliance by Design
slides.col1:
Legal Framework
* Token-to-title linkage via smart contract and off-chain registry
* Notarial witnessing of transfer
* Qatar Civil Code and real estate law alignment
* Regulatory sandbox potential
slides.col2:
DALP Controls
* 12 compliance modules enforced on-chain
* Country Allow/Block Lists for jurisdiction
* Identity verification for all parties
* Transfer Approval for manual override
* Collateral module for encumbrances

slides.title: Compliance Policy
slides.body: Pre-configured compliance modules with country restrictions, identity verification, investor limits, and transfer controls
slides.image_path: ../shared/brand/dalp-screenshots/14 - Compliance and Policy Templates/jpg/Policy Template - Qatar Real Estate Token - Overview.jpg

slides.title: Risk Control Matrix
slides.body: The following matrix maps the primary risks associated with tokenized real estate settlement to the specific DALP controls that address each one. Risk severity reflects the potential impact on settlement integrity, regulatory standing, and participant confidence. Each mitigation control is already available within the DALP platform and does not require custom development.

slides.title: Disputes and Exceptions
slides.body:
* Failed Settlement: Assets remain locked in the XvP contract until a cancellation vote passes or the settlement expires past its cutoff date.
* Cancellation: Multi-party voting is required. All parties must consent before assets are returned to their original holders.
* Expired Settlement: Assets become automatically available for withdrawal once the cutoff date passes without execution.
* Approval Revocation: Any party can revoke their approval before execution, unlocking their assets from the settlement contract.
* Dispute Resolution: The platform provides a complete audit trail of every action. Off-chain arbitration can reference on-chain evidence.
* HTLC Timeout: If the hashlock secret is not revealed before the expiry window, all parties can withdraw their locked assets.

slides.title: Asset Lifecycle View
slides.body: Real-time view of tokenized real estate assets with full event history, verification status, and integrated data feeds. Track ownership transfers, compliance events, and valuation updates across the entire asset lifecycle from issuance through settlement and redemption.
slides.image_path: ../shared/brand/dalp-screenshots/12 - Real Estate/jpg/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Asset Details.jpg

slides.title: Measurable Impact
slides.stat1_body: From weeks of manual escrow to near-instant atomic settlement
slides.stat2_body: Fewer intermediaries, automated compliance, reduced reconciliation
slides.stat3_body: Atomic XvP eliminates delivery vs payment mismatch
slides.stat4_body: Every approval, transfer, and state change recorded on-chain

slides.title: Phased Rollout
slides.milestone1_body: Requirements, legal framework alignment, stakeholder mapping
slides.milestone2_body: DALP deployment, token configuration, XvP setup, integration testing
slides.milestone3_body: 5-10 test transactions with real parties, regulatory review
slides.milestone4_body: Full deployment, training, bank integration, additional asset types, cross-border

slides.title: Operations Dashboard
slides.body: Centralized monitoring across all tokenized assets, active settlements, compliance events, and platform metrics. The dashboard provides geographic distribution, portfolio analytics, and real-time alerts for settlement milestones, compliance breaches, and system health indicators.

slides.title: Strategic Recommendations
slides.col1:
For Ministry of Justice
* Lead digital transformation of property transfer
* Reduce fraud risk with immutable records
* Enable faster, cheaper real estate transactions
slides.col2:
For Banks
* New revenue from tokenized deposit issuance
* Position as settlement infrastructure provider
* Reduce operational cost of escrow management
slides.col3:
For the Market
* Shorter settlement attracts foreign investment
* Transparency builds market confidence
* Foundation for broader asset tokenization
